tipRules.tip: KVD: Position boat outside the grass line, fire at a 45° quartering angle into shallower grass. Crank down into the grass, slow up — let the bait float free or rip it free. That stop-start-rip action through grass is 'almost as good as a finesse bait at triggering inactive fish.'

newStructureAdvice.grass: KVD: '45° angle trick' — position outside the grass line, cast quartering into shallower grass. The crankbait follows the contour/taper of the grass line as it comes back to you. You can feel every irregularity — subtle points, cuts, where grass comes out farther or gets taller. Fish relate to those irregularities. Mark the spot when you get bit — they won't be alone.

knowledge.insight: KVD: Position boat just outside the grass line. Cast at 45° quartering angle into shallower grass. Crank down until you hit grass, then slow up. If it won't float free, snap it out. The bait follows the contour of the grass as it comes back to the deeper edge where your boat sits. This is the most effective way to cover a lot of grass quickly.

KVD: When crankbait sticks in grass — if it won't come through, snap it free. Otherwise let it float off. The moment it tears free or floats off is when you get the bite. 'A crankbait through the grass is almost as good as a finesse bait like a worm at triggering strikes from inactive fish.'

KVD: Fluorocarbon is mandatory for cranking grass — low stretch helps rip bait free. Soft-tip composite rod (glass/graphite blend) for feel and power. Multiple rods with different line sizes. Series 5 crankbait runs 10-12ft — wants a bait that runs to the edge of the grass, not one that buries into it.

KVD: The crankbait is a search tool for reading the grass line — you can feel where grass comes out farther, where it's taller, subtle points and cuts. Fish relate to irregularities in the grass edge. On natural lakes where structure is subtle, the crankbait finds what your eyes can't.
